sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Mallorca have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, managing 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, with a notable 1-0 victory over Getafe. Osasuna, on the other hand, are in poor form, failing to win in their last 3 league games (2 losses, 1 draw), with a heavy 1-3 defeat to Real Sociedad. This gives Mallorca a slight edge going into the match at home.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Mallorca and Osasuna have had tight battles, with a 1-1 draw in February 2025 and a 1-0 Osasuna win in August 2024. Historically, matches between these teams are low-scoring, with under 2.5 goals in 6 of their last 7 meetings, suggesting a cautious, defensive game could be on the cards.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Mallorca, sitting 16th with 12 points, have struggled offensively with 13 goals scored but are decent in aerial duels (54.4% won, 5th in league). Osasuna, 17th with 11 points, have scored only 10 goals this season and are missing key players like Ante Budimir, which could hamper their attack. Mallorca's home advantage and slightly better form may play a decisive role.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Mallorca's Vedat Muriqi (6 goals, 5th in league) will be crucial for their attack, while Abdon Prats is in form with a 7.75 average rating over the last 5 matches. For Osasuna, goalkeeper Sergio Herrera (7.8 average rating) could be vital in keeping the scoreline tight, especially with their defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Mallorca's home advantage, slightly better recent form, and Osasuna's struggles in attack, a narrow win for Mallorca seems likely. Sofascore's model supports this with a 43% chance of a home win compared to 23% for an away win. Expect a low-scoring game with under 2.5 goals, consistent with head-to-head trends.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Mallorca and Osasuna ended in a goalless draw at Estadi de Son Moix. Neither team managed to break the deadlock despite some promising moments. Mallorca dominated possession and showed more intent in the attacking areas, while Osasuna struggled to create clear-cut chances. Both teams displayed a physical approach, as evidenced by the equal number of yellow cards received. The game remains finely balanced as we head into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Mallorca's first half performance
halftimeSections.text: Mallorca controlled much of the game in the first half, holding a significant share of possession and making frequent entries into the final third. Their ability to win duels, especially in the air, allowed them to maintain pressure on Osasuna's defense. While they managed a shot on target, they lacked the cutting edge in the final moments to convert their dominance into goals. Players like Samú Costa and Vedat Muriqi were central to their efforts, with the latter showing promise in linking up play.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Osasuna's first half performance
halftimeSections.text: Osasuna found themselves on the back foot for large parts of the first half, with limited possession and fewer entries into dangerous areas. Their defensive structure held firm, but they struggled to win physical battles and were often outmaneuvered in duels. Despite this, they showed glimpses of threat, particularly in isolated moments of transition. Their expected goals figure suggests they had potential to score, but a lack of shots on target highlights their difficulty in creating real danger.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half saw Mallorca take the initiative early on, with consistent attacking spells that pinned Osasuna back. There were brief periods where Osasuna pushed forward, particularly around the middle of the half, showing intent to counter. However, Mallorca quickly regained control with sustained pressure as the half progressed. Towards the end, Osasuna had a notable attacking spell, but Mallorca finished the half strongly with renewed momentum.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas to watch in the second half
halftimeSections.text: For Mallorca, the key will be to maintain their dominance in possession and convert their territorial advantage into goals. They need to be more clinical in the penalty area, where they’ve already made several entries. Osasuna, on the other hand, must improve their duel-winning ability and look to exploit transitions more effectively. Their defensive resilience will be tested if Mallorca continues to press. Set pieces could also play a role, given the physicality both teams have shown.
